title: Optimize performance bottlenecks across 4-file data processing system

result.details:
=== Part 1: Functional Correctness Tests (20 points) ===
=== data_loader.py tests ===
PASS loader_basic
PASS loader_dedup
PASS loader_field_values
PASS loader_consistency
PASS loader_empty

=== processor.py tests ===
PASS processor_fields
PASS processor_tier_logic
PASS processor_summary_lines
PASS processor_filter
FAIL processor_normalized: normalized amount avg per category should be ~1.0

=== indexer.py tests ===
PASS indexer_count
PASS indexer_lookup
PASS indexer_lookup_missing
FAIL indexer_unique: expected 5 sorted categories, got ['electronics', 'clothing', 'food', 'books', 'toys']
FAIL indexer_remove: removed=None, count=80

=== reporter.py tests ===
PASS reporter_sorted_count
Traceback (most recent call last):
  File "/tmp/task_frontier_perf_optimization_4file_7f_zj9tp/tests/test_correctness.py", line 325, in <module>
    test_reporter_top_n()
  File "/tmp/task_frontier_perf_optimization_4file_7f_zj9tp/tests/test_correctness.py", line 253, in test_reporter_top_n
    report = rpt.generate_report(sort_field="amount", reverse=True)
TypeError: Reporter.generate_report() got an unexpected keyword argument 'sort_field'

Functional tests: 13 / 20

=== Part 2: Structural / Pattern Checks (8 points) ===
PASS struct_no_nested_dedup: No nested loop in deduplicate
PASS struct_loader_caching: data_loader has caching mechanism
FAIL struct_no_per_record_avg: category averages still recomputed per record
PASS struct_no_json_copy: No json serialization for copying
PASS struct_uses_join: build_summary_text uses join
PASS struct_dict_index: indexer uses dict-based lookup
PASS struct_no_resort: add_record doesn't re-sort entire list
PASS struct_no_redundant_lists: Redundant list copies eliminated

=== Part 3: Performance / Timing Checks (2 points) ===
PASS timing_5k_pipeline: 5000 records in 0.050s (limit 3.0s)
PASS timing_index_lookup: 10000 lookups in 0.002s (limit 1.0s)

=== Summary ===
Functional: 13 / 20
Score: 22/30
